name: tabs
title: Tabs
description: An APG tabs pattern with roving tabindex, automatic or manual activation, Home/End support, disabled-item skipping, RTL-aware horizontal arrows, and a selected state conveyed by structure and weight as well as color.
useWhen: Switching between a small set of peer views within the same context without navigating to a new page
The tab labels are short, distinct, and the panel content is available immediately
People benefit from comparing related categories while preserving the surrounding page context
avoidWhen: The sections should all be visible or discoverable by document search — use headings
The choices are steps in a process — use a stepper or separate pages
There are too many labels to fit or understand at a glance — use navigation or a select
Changing tabs triggers a slow network request — use manual activation or ordinary links

keyboard: Tab enters the tablist on the selected tab and then moves into the open panel
ArrowLeft and ArrowRight move through horizontal tabs, wrap at the ends, skip disabled tabs, and flip meaning in RTL
ArrowUp and ArrowDown move through vertical tabs and wrap at the ends
Home and End move to the first and last enabled tab
With automatic activation (default), moving focus selects the tab; with activateOnFocus=false, Enter or Space selects it
Selection is exposed with aria-selected and shown with font weight plus a 2px logical-edge indicator, never color alone
